_help.purpose: Auto-scoring thresholds for content performance. Read by scripts/pattern-miner.py (and future /weekly-scorecard) to assign CRUSH/SOLID/MEH/MISS ratings from platform metrics. Taylor's explicit taylor_rating column in performance-ledger.csv ALWAYS wins over auto-score.
_help.tuning: Edit this file to adjust thresholds. Changes take effect next run — no code edits required.
_help.source_format: JSON (not YAML) because system python3 is 3.9 with no PyYAML. Stdlib only.

calendar_weights._comment: 60% from pattern-miner winners, 30% new angles on proven pillars, 10% experiments

youtube._comment: Re-tune channel_avg_views quarterly or when audience shifts. Used as denominator for views_multiple.

shorts._comment: Shorts have different dynamics — separate thresholds

beehiiv._comment: 2026 real-estate newsletter benchmarks. Avg open: 30-40%, top 10%: 45%+. Avg click: 2-4%, top 10%: 8%+.

tiktok._comment: Placeholder — real thresholds after TrendsMCP data seeds

attribution_priority._comment: When multiple signals point to different ratings, priority wins top-down.

pattern_miner._comment: Group CRUSH rows by these dimensions to find winning patterns. Combinations with >= min_sample_size CRUSH instances become patterns. Dimensions must match enriched-row field names in pattern-miner.py.
